description:
# Why

bl-38dd added `.balls/tasks` symlink creation to `state_repo::ensure` so
master_url-configured repos get the same convenience symlink the legacy
leg drops in `setup_state_branch`. The target differs between the two
layouts: `worktree/.balls/tasks` (legacy) vs `state-repo/.balls/tasks`
(master_url).

`ensure_tasks_symlink` short-circuits on `is_symlink()` without
inspecting the target, so a repo that was initialized in legacy mode
and later flipped to master_url via `bl remaster --commit <url>` keeps
its old symlink pointing at `worktree/.balls/tasks`. If
`.balls/worktree/` is gone (which it should be in master_url mode), the
symlink dangles silently. bl itself keeps working because Store routes
through `tasks_dir_path`, but the README-advertised ergonomic of
`ls .balls/tasks/` breaks.

# Scope

In `ensure_tasks_symlink` (or its master_url caller in
`state_repo::ensure`), if the existing symlink's `read_link` target
disagrees with the expected target, repoint it. The bl-init guard
against non-symlinks at the path is unaffected — a stray directory or
file there still aborts. Idempotency is preserved because matching
targets are still no-ops.

`bl remaster` itself is a candidate site too — repointing the symlink
as part of the commit step would mean `state_repo::ensure`'s call only
ever runs in the freshly-correct state. Pick whichever matches the
project's preference between defensive (in ensure) and proactive (in
remaster).

# Out of scope

The reverse direction (master_url → legacy via `bl remaster --detach`)
has the same shape but is rarer and easier to spot. Same fix applies.

# Origin

bl-38dd review pass — sibling concern flagged at delivery, captured to
keep the symlink work self-contained.
title: Stale .balls/tasks symlink survives legacy → master_url remaster
